#cloud8 #azure 
## Introduction
  
The CSP allows partners to transact across Microsoft cloud services through a single platform. This option allows partners to monitor the customer lifecycle, set pricing and terms, and bill directly. Additionally, it allows partners to provision and manage subscriptions.

Cloud8's integration for partners with CSP customer accounts has two ways to consume cost data:

1. Configuring App Registration with a Reader Role in each CSP Customer's Azure Subscription;
    
2. Configuring App Registration with Reader Role in CSP Partner Subscription
    
 We suggest you open a notepad and be ready to write down the following information:

  - SUBSCRIPTION ID (Step 1)
    
- SECRET VALUE (Step 2)
    
- APPLICATION ID (Step 2)
    
- TENANT ID (Step 2) 
 
## Cloud Solution Provider - Customer Provider Configuration

### Step 1 - Defining the CSP Customer Provider Subscription

Search for “Subscription” on top search bar.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Choose the subscription and save the Subscription ID.
### Step 2 - Configuring App Registration

Search for Microsoft Entra ID, and click on App Registrations.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Click on “New registration” and choose a name.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Select the App Registration you just created and click Certificates & Secrets in the left-hand menu. Then click New Client Secret. Choose a name for the key and set the expiration date to 24 months.

Once created, the provider will be configured using the Secret Value. Make a note of it immediately after creation, as it will no longer be visible.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Still in App Registration, save the Application ID and Tenant ID values.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Step 3 - Associate the necessary permissions with App Registration

Search for the Subscription again, then click on Access control (IAM) in the left side menu of the selected Subscription, and then on Add > Add role assignment.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Select the Reader Role, and click Next:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

On the Members tab, click Select members, and then search for the name of the App Registration you created. Then click Review + assign:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

That's it! You've now created your Service Principal and are ready to associate it with Cloud8. Fill in the parameters collected in the previous steps.

- Subscription ID = SUBSCRIPTION ID
    
- Tenant ID = TENANT ID
    
- Application ID = APPLICATION ID
    
- Senha = SECRET VALUE
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Cloud Solution Provider - Partner Provider Configuration

### Step 1 - Defining the CSP Customer Provider Subscription

Search for "Subscription" – Access the CSP Partner Subscription.

Under Cost Management + Billing, select the billing scope where the Billing Account Type is "Microsoft Partner Agreement." Note the Partner Billing Scope Name.
 

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Step 2 - Validate if App Registration is configured

Search for Microsoft Entra ID and click App Registrations.

Then, check to see if there are already any App Registrations registered for Cloud8. If so, note the following:

- Enterprise Application Name
    
- Enterprise Application ID
    
- Enterprise Application Object ID
    

Proceed to Step 4 to continue configuring your provider.

### Step 3 - Configuring App Registration

Search for Microsoft Entra ID, and click App Registrations.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  

Click on “New registration” and choose a name.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Select the App Registration you just created and click Certificates & Secrets in the left-hand menu. Then click New Client Secret. Choose a name for the key and set the expiration date to 24 months.

Once created, the provider will be configured using the Secret Value. Make a note of it immediately after creation, as it will no longer be visible.

  

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Still in App Registration, save the Application ID and Tenant ID values.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  

### Step 4 - Associate the necessary permissions with App Registration

  

Search for the Subscription again, then click on Access control (IAM) in the left side menu of the selected Subscription, and then on Add > Add role assignment.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  

Select the Reader Role, and click Next:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

On the Members tab, click Select members, and then search for the name of the App Registration you created. Then click Review + assign:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

That's it! You've now created your Service Principal and are ready to associate it with Cloud8. Note down the following information:

- Part Tenant Name
    
- Part Tenant ID
    
- App Registration Name
    
- App Registration ID
    
- App Registration Object ID
    
- Secret Name
    
- Secret ID
    
- Secret Value
    

Send the data to [suporte@cloud8.com.br](mailto:suporte@cloud8.com.br), requesting the configuration of the Partner provider by providing the above data.

____________________________________________________________________________
# Setting up FinOps Analytics on Cloud8

FinOps Analytics configuration is done through connection to App Registration.
# Enabling Best Practices on Cloud8

Once FinOps Analytics is configured and data is synchronized with Cloud8, you can enable the Best Practices feature in Cloud8.

Best Practices is an advanced advisor that combines over 1,000 unique security, backup, compliance, and cost reduction rules for AWS, Azure, GCP, and OCI with flexible alerts via Teams, Slack, or email. 

In the Cloud8 side menu, select Providers. Select the desired provider and click "Best Practices."

![](data:image/png;base64,)

You must select the providers on which you want to enable the functionality, and to do so, uncheck the “Disabled on this provider” checkbox and select the “Same as main credentials” option.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Then click "Configure."

Note: If FinOps Analytics has just been enabled, you will need to wait at least 24 hours before enabling the Best Practices feature.
